relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Wives are addressed within a difficult household world, with conduct as witness.
Pure conduct in reverence is seen.
Adornment is moved away from display.
The hidden heart becomes precious through gentle quiet spirit.
Holy women hoped in the Divine.
Sarah is invoked with doing good and no terror.
Husbands must honor wives as co-heirs of life-grace.
All are called into sympathy, sibling-love, tenderness, and humility.
Do not repay harm with harm; bless instead.
Loving life begins with guarding speech.
Turn from harm, do good, seek peace.
The Lord hears the just and faces harm-doers.
Zeal for good is the path.
Suffering for justice is blessed, but fear is not lord.
Set Messiah apart in the heart and answer hope with gentleness.
Good conscience protects beautiful conduct.
Doing good remains better than doing harm.
Messiah suffered to bring us to the Divine.
In Spirit he proclaimed to imprisoned spirits.
Noah’s ark becomes a remembered pattern of rescue through water.
Baptism is appeal of good conscience through resurrection.
Messiah reigns at Divine right hand above powers.

contemplative_note: 1 Peter 3 is hope without fear: difficult household lines are guarded, husbands must honor co-heirs, and every defense of hope must be gentle.
